channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: TikTok no longer accessible in US
item.description: TikTok blocked access to American users late Saturday night, just hours before a law banning the popular video-sharing platform was set to go into effect. “A law banning TikTok has been enacted in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Trump says he ‘most likely’ will give TikTok 90-day extension
item.description: President-elect Trump told NBC News on Saturday that he is likely to give TikTok a 90-day extension while his incoming administration assesses the situation following the Supreme Court decision to uphold a federal ban.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Racing against time on a menacing caldera: survey finds majority of organizations take days to tackle critical vulnerabilities, each of them a potential open goal for cybercriminals
item.description: Over half of organizations lack effective vulnerability management, with 73% fearing penalties and operational gaps increasing risks of breaches and regulatory scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: These powerful in-wheel motors could spawn the next generation of affordable high-performance EVs
item.description: Donut Lab recently showcased the next generation of its in-wheel motor technology that has the potential to change the EV game.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: For TikTok users, mourning, frustration and clinging to hope as TikTok ban looms
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The U.S. is inching closer and closer to a potential TikTok ban — with the nation's highest court upholding a law that's set to halt new downloads of the app starting Sunday.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and MAE ANDERSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Carnahan accomplished much of what she came to GSA to do
item.description: Robin Carnahan, the out-going GSA administrator, said the federal footprint shrunk by 11 million square feet and saved $2 billion in 2024 alone. The post Carnahan accomplished much of what she came to GSA to do first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Who could buy TikTok? Only a few parties are serious about offering
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The supremely popular TikTok could be banned on Jan. 19 under a federal law that forces the video sharing platform to divest itself from its China-based parent company, ByteDance, or shut down its U.S. operations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: TikTok refugees are pouring to Xiaohongshu. Here's what you need to know about the RedNote app
item.description: A rare wave of U.S.-China camaraderie broke out online in recent days as “refugees” from the popular short video platform TikTok poured onto a Chinese social media platform to protest a now-delayed ban on the service.
item.dc:creator: Fu Ting And David Cohen, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hackers hide malware into website images to go unnoticed
item.description: Multiple groups are using the same infection chain to deliver different infostealers, including 0bj3ctivityStealer and VIP Keylogger.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: What kind of mini PC is that? Minisforum's NAS packs a Ryzen AI HX 370, up to 96GB RAM and a staggering 154TB storage
item.description: Mini PC maker Minisforum reveals its N5 Pro NAS device, powered by a Ryzen AI HX 370 CPU and with a staggering 154TB of storage.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: How Apple is in the race for workplace AI
item.description: In a few years, every new employee entering the workforce will already have become accustomed to using AI to solve problems and help with tasks – and they’re going to want the same tools at work as those they use at home.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Border app that became ‘a salvation’ for migrants to legally enter the US may end
item.description: CBP One has brought nearly 1 million people to the U.S. on two-year permits with eligibility to work but could go away once President-elect Donald Trump takes office.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Oracle updates Exadata systems to speed database operations
item.description: If you had forgotten that Oracle was in the hardware business, it’s easy to understand why, as Oracle has not exactly promoted the business very well. Oracle’s hardware is descended from Sun Microsystems servers, which Oracle acquired in 2010.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Xiaomi tipped to set impressive new phone battery benchmark in 2025 – and I hope Apple and Samsung are taking notes
item.description: Xiaomi is rumored to be working on a new phone with a 7,500mAh battery, which would be the largest battery ever fitted to a conventional smartphone, and I hope Apple, Samsung, and Google are paying attention.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Over a million WordPress sites exposed to attack from W3 Total Cache plugin flaw
item.description: A recently discovered WordPress plugin flaw allows threat actors to access sensitive information, run unauthorized actions, and more.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: TikTok ban upheld by Supreme Court
item.description: TikTok and ByteDance — along with some content creators and users — argued the law violates constitutionally protected free speech rights.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: TikTok among six tech firms under fire for sending Europeans' personal data to China
item.description: Temu, Xiaomi, AliExpress, Shein, WeChat, and TikTok are in breach of EU laws, say experts. Here's what we know so far.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Biden won't enforce TikTok ban, official says, leaving fate of app to Trump
item.description: President Joe Biden won't enforce a ban on the social media app TikTok that is set to take effect a day before he leaves office on Monday, a U.S. official said Thursday, leaving its fate in the hands of President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Nvidia presenta nuevos microservicios de protección para la IA agéntica
item.description: Nvidia ha incorporado a sus herramientas de software Nvidia NeMo Guardrails nuevos microservicios de inferencia (NIM) para robustecer la seguridad de la inteligencia artificial (IA).
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 3 big predictions for AI in financial services in 2025
item.description: As we look ahead to 2025, the financial services landscape will be shaped not by AI as a standalone innovation, but by how it enables smarter, faster, and more personalized experiences. Here are three key predictions for how AI will transform the industry: 1.
item.dc:creator: Ben Borodach, Fast Company

item.title: Here’s how Google is using LLMs for complex internal code migrations
item.description: Code migration is a critical process in maintaining software applications. It helps improve performance and resilience, keeps systems up to date, and removes stale or irrelevant code.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Biden’s clean AI infrastructure plan could be hanging by a thread
item.description: An executive order from outgoing US president Joe Biden aimed at advancing the nation’s leadership in AI infrastructure is “welcome and needed in theory, but this one seems rushed and somewhat at odds with the order just passed on AI chip export restrictions,” an analyst said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: President Biden’s new executive order aims to bolster national security
item.description: President Joe Biden issued an executive order on Thursday aimed at strengthening the nation’s cybersecurity and making it easier to go after foreign adversaries or hacking groups that try to compromise U.S. internet and telecommunication systems.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Apple satellite patent takes big step toward stable communication
item.description: Apple has big plans for satellite services, a new patent filing confirms. It’s the latest nugget of information to roll down Apple Confidential mountain, a plan to make it much easier for devices to maintain connectivity as they move between satellites.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Snowflake open sources SwiftKV to reduce inference workload costs
item.description: Cloud-based data warehouse company Snowflake has open-sourced a new proprietary approach — SwiftKV — designed to reduce the cost of inference workloads for enterprises running generative AI-based applications. SwiftKV was launched in December.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: From Safety to Accountability: The Game-Changing Impact of Kazakhstan’s Surveillance System
item.description: Technology innovations have made the digital transformation of public services essential to enhancing safety, efficiency, and accountability.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Powerful Webb Telescope captures photos of one of the earliest supernova ever seen
item.description: The James Webb Space Telescope captured photos of one of the earliest supernovas ever seen using infrared technology, and creating a time lapse of the phenomena.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The death of DEI in tech
item.description: Save me from rich, white men who insist they and their kind are being discriminated against. Tech entrepreneur Elon Musk, majority owner (not the founder) of SpaceX, Tesla, and numerous other leading companies, insists that “DEI [Diversity, equity, and inclusion] is just another word for racism.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg will cohost reception with Republican billionaires for Trump inauguration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg is cohosting a reception with billionaire Republican donors next week for Donald Trump’s inauguration, the latest sign of the Facebook founder's embrace of the president-elect.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Court lets Tennessee porn site age verification law take effect as Texas law goes to Supreme Court
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — A panel of appeals judges has ruled that Tennessee can begin enforcing a law that requires pornographic websites to verify their visitors’ age as the First Amendment debate rises to the U.S. Supreme Court this week for oral arguments over a similar Texas law.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Technology executives on the move at Education, OPM, HHS
item.description: Steve Hernandez, Education’s CISO, and Brian Bordelon, Education’s deputy CIO, have moved to new agencies to take on similar roles. The post Technology executives on the move at Education, OPM, HHS first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: New Brazil law restricts use of smartphones in elementary and high schools
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazil's President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva on Monday signed a bill restricting the use of smartphones at school, following a global trend for such limitations. The move will impact students at elementary and high schools across the South American nation starting in February.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE and DIARLEI RODRIGUES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 5-minute cleanup for your phone and computer
item.description: Technology expert Kim Komando shares her easy tips on how to declutter your phones, computers and devices from the files you no longer need.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI experts tamp down hype, expect challenges for 2025
item.description: A trio of AI researchers and experts in Lithuania and England have tossed some cold water on the hype surrounding generative AI and predict that in 2025, companies and investors will have to lower their expectations regarding what the technology and large language models (LLM) can do.
item.dc:creator: Network World
